Finding: NCR-adjacent records (Out-of-Spec Reaction, Part Fallout Log) have no NCR number/unique tracking ID on the form.
Impact: Events can’t be tied to a single case record; trending and closure tracking become manual and unreliable.
Evidence: 8.2.1.2 ECoat Line Forms/8.2.1.2.10 Out of Specification Reaction Form.xlsx | Sheet 1 D1='OUT OF SPECIFICATION REACTION FORM' (no NCR/ID field found); 8.2.1.2 ECoat Line Forms/8.2.1.2.29 Part Fallout log.xlsx | Sheet1 A2='PART FALLOUT LOG' (no NCR field found)

Finding: Root cause / corrective action capture is not integrated into these reaction logs; at best there are adjustment notes.
Impact: No closed-loop IATF-style containment→cause→verification without creating separate paperwork or tribal knowledge.
Evidence: 8.2.1.2 ECoat Line Forms/8.2.1.2.10 Out of Specification Reaction Form.xlsx | Sheet 1 D1='OUT OF SPECIFICATION REACTION FORM' (no NCR/ID field found)

Finding: Load identifiers exist on load sheets (load bar color run start/end) but are not carried into defect/inspection tracking forms.
Impact: Complaint trace-back breaks between production tracking and inspection/defect logs.
Evidence: 8.2.1.1 Powder Line Forms/8.2.1.1.9 Part Load Sheet.xls.xlsx | Sheet 1 H5='Starting Load Bar of Colour Run:' (load id exists here but not on defect/inspection forms)

Finding: GLDC inspection records capture defect location codes (e.g., A6, R4 BP) but not a rack/load/run identifier.
Impact: You can’t link defects to a specific production run or process parameters without guesswork.
Evidence: 8.2.1.3 Inspection and Testing Forms and Labels/8.2.1.3.34 GLDC/Scanned Copies/March 15 2024_005.jpg | Defect grid records location codes (e.g., 'A6', 'R4 BP') not counts

Finding: Operator identity is often recorded as initials only.
Impact: Can’t reliably link defects to qualified individuals or build accountability/competency analytics.
Evidence: 8.2.1.3 Inspection and Testing Forms and Labels/8.2.1.3.1 General/8.2.1.3.1.5 Powder Defect Tracking Sheet.xlsx | Sheet DEFECT TRACKING S3='Operator Initials'; 8.2.1.1 Powder Line Forms/8.2.1.1.9 Part Load Sheet.xls.xlsx | 'Operator Initials'

Finding: Customer-specific defect tracking sheets use different defect lists (e.g., 'Paint in Threads' vs 'Pinholes' vs 'Sanding marks').
Impact: Plant-level Pareto and cross-customer trend analysis requires manual normalization.
Evidence: Examples: 8.2.1.3.1.5 (Paint in Threads); 8.2.1.3.6.12 (Pinholes, Sanding marks)

Finding: Free-text 'Other (Define Defect)' buckets exist on core defect tracking sheets.
Impact: Data becomes non-aggregatable; analysts end up parsing text instead of using controlled taxonomy.
Evidence: 8.2.1.3 Inspection and Testing Forms and Labels/8.2.1.3.1 General/8.2.1.3.1.5 Powder Defect Tracking Sheet.xlsx | Sheet DEFECT TRACKING N3='Other (Define Defect)'

Finding: Defect tracking forms list defect types but don’t consistently capture denominators (total loaded/inspected) on the same record.
Impact: PPM and first-pass yield cannot be calculated reliably from the defect log alone.
Evidence: 8.2.1.3 Inspection and Testing Forms and Labels/8.2.1.3.1 General/8.2.1.3.1.5 Powder Defect Tracking Sheet.xlsx | Sheet DEFECT TRACKING Row 3 (defect columns + rework/scrap columns; no qty inspected/loaded fields)

Finding: Some inspection records emphasize defect location marking rather than count-per-defect.
Impact: Quantitative defect rates become impossible without rework.
Evidence: 8.2.1.3 Inspection and Testing Forms and Labels/8.2.1.3.34 GLDC/Scanned Copies/March 15 2024_005.jpg | Defect grid records location codes (e.g., 'A6', 'R4 BP') not counts

Finding: Disposition is captured as ad-hoc columns like 'Repaired @ Line', 'Sent To Rework', 'Scrapped'—not mapped to standard DispositionCodes.
Impact: Disposition reporting and approval logic won’t be consistent; scrap/rework totals drift.
Evidence: 8.2.1.3 Inspection and Testing Forms and Labels/8.2.1.3.1 General/8.2.1.3.1.5 Powder Defect Tracking Sheet.xlsx | Sheet DEFECT TRACKING Q3='Scrapped' (also Rework columns on same header row)

Finding: No evidence that disposition requires customer approval gate in the same record (when required).
Impact: Risk of shipping unapproved deviations or losing approval evidence.
Evidence: 8.2.1.3 Inspection and Testing Forms and Labels/8.2.1.3.1 General/8.2.1.3.1.10 Certification Labels.docx + GP12 docs (no approval ID); 8.2.1.3 Inspection and Testing Forms and Labels/8.2.1.3.1 General/8.2.1.3.1.13 GP12 Flow.docx

Finding: Many forms are stale (older than 2 years; some since 2016) with no enforced retirement process visible in package.
Impact: Obsolete work instructions and outdated requirements can remain in circulation.
Evidence: ai_indexes/forms_manifest.csv | 43 files have last_modified_utc older than 2 years (as-of 2026-02-24); some as old as 2016

Finding: Duplicate/superseded clusters exist (e.g., '-edit' copies).
Impact: Users can pick the wrong template; revision control is not trustworthy.
Evidence: ai_indexes/duplicate_revision_map.csv | 5 duplicate/superseded clusters (including '-edit' copies)

Finding: Some Excel templates are structurally hostile (extreme merged cell usage).
Impact: Automated migration and reliable UI replication become expensive; errors likely.
Evidence: 8.2.1.2 ECoat Line Forms/8.2.1.2.24 BASF PB Ratio Dennis.xlsx | merged cell ranges ≈ 69538 (hostile to digital parsing)

Finding: Legacy .xls files are present and not parsed in the package index.
Impact: Evidence gaps; high chance critical records live in formats that won’t migrate cleanly.
Evidence: Multiple .xls marked LEGACY_XLS_NOT_PARSED in ai_indexes/sheet_index.csv (e.g., 8.2.1.1.17, 8.2.1.1.18)

Finding: Scrap/rework decisions are recorded but not tied to cost fields (labor, material, customer chargeback) on the same record.
Impact: Cost-of-poor-quality requires manual reconciliation.
Evidence: 8.2.1.3 Inspection and Testing Forms and Labels/8.2.1.3.1 General/8.2.1.3.1.5 Powder Defect Tracking Sheet.xlsx | Sheet DEFECT TRACKING Q3='Scrapped'; 8.2.1.2 ECoat Line Forms/8.2.1.2.29 Part Fallout log.xlsx | Sheet1 A2='PART FALLOUT LOG' (no NCR field found) (no cost fields found)

Finding: Operator/inspector identity is inconsistent and not referenceable (initials, sometimes missing).
Impact: No way to enforce qualification gating or analyze defect vs competency.
Evidence: 8.2.1.3 Inspection and Testing Forms and Labels/8.2.1.3.1 General/8.2.1.3.1.5 Powder Defect Tracking Sheet.xlsx | Operator Initials; 8.2.1.1 Powder Line Forms/8.2.1.1.9 Part Load Sheet.xls.xlsx | Operator Initials

Finding: Inspection/test records don’t capture instrument ID/cal status (film build, adhesion etc.).
Impact: False acceptance risk and inability to defend measurements during audits/complaints.
Evidence: 8.2.1.3 Inspection and Testing Forms and Labels/8.2.1.3.1 General/8.2.1.3.1.4 Powder General Inspection and Testing.xlsx | Sheet1 I2 instruction for film build readings but no gauge/calibration reference field detected

Finding: Calibration evidence exists (gauge check logs) but is not linked to the measurement results that depend on it.
Impact: You can’t prove measurement validity for a given inspection record.
Evidence: 8.2.1.1 Powder Line Forms/8.2.1.1.29 Offline Booth Pressure Differential Gauge Check Sheet.xlsx | 'Pressure Differential Gauge Check Sheet' includes DATE/INITIALS but not linked to any inspection/test record

Finding: Certification labels capture date/part/operator but no lot/load/run linkage.
Impact: Shipment release evidence can’t be tied back to production/inspection records.
Evidence: 8.2.1.3 Inspection and Testing Forms and Labels/8.2.1.3.1 General/8.2.1.3.1.10 Certification Labels.docx | DOCX label contains DATE, PART #, OPERATOR, REASON; no Lot/Run/Load ID

Finding: GP-12 flow exists as a document but there’s no traceable GP-12 state per lot/run in the records.
Impact: Early production containment can’t be audited digitally; exit criteria may be missed.
Evidence: 8.2.1.3 Inspection and Testing Forms and Labels/8.2.1.3.1 General/8.2.1.3.1.13 GP12 Flow.docx | GP12 Flow doc references tagging/nonconforming handling but no record identifier / linkage mechanism
